summary.text: AI Agent frameworks continue to dominate developer attention, with MiroFish (64.5k stars, +2.2k in 24h) and Vibe-Trading (10.8k stars, +886 in 24h) showing sustained high velocity. The category accounts for 4 of the top projects, averaging 784 daily stars. Coding/SWE agents also remain strong, averaging 2,108 daily stars. A notable acceleration anomaly is CowAgent, which jumped 2.3x in daily delta (33 stars) relative to its weekly average, suggesting a breakout. On Hacker News, discussions around AI self-improvement and the impact of AI on education signal growing developer introspection. The cross-source confirmation of two projects (Open Code Review and Anthropic's vulnerability framework) appearing on both GitHub and HN reinforces interest in AI-assisted code review and security. The absence of new local LLM or RAG breakout projects suggests attention has shifted toward agentic and security tooling.

projects.name: Vibe-Trading

llmSummary.oneLiner: Vibe-Trading is an open-source, LLM-powered personal trading agent that connects to multiple brokers and data sources for automated, paper-trading and live-trading strategies.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It provides a unified, agentic interface for algorithmic trading, allowing users to research, backtest, and execute trades across various brokers (IBKR, Robinhood, Tiger, Alpaca, OKX, Binance, Futu) using natural language commands and LLM-driven decision-making, while incorporating safety guards like mandates and kill switches.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 10,771 stars, 886 stars in the last 24 hours, and a sustained_hot trend, Vibe-Trading is experiencing rapid, organic growth. The project has a high rule-based score of 70, recent releases, and active commits within 7 days, indicating strong community interest and ongoing development. The acceleration ratio is low (1), suggesting steady momentum rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable project to watch for AI-driven fintech innovation.
llmSummary.whatChanged: Recent updates include a local data cache for all 7 data sources (June 4), six new broker connectors (Tiger, Longbridge, Alpaca, OKX, Binance, Futu) with paper trading and bounded order placement (June 2), and a v0.1.9 release (June 1) with connector-first broker profiles, research goal runtime, and 36 MCP tools. These features significantly expand the platform's utility and safety, likely driving the surge in stars.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Vibe-Trading combines the hottest trends in AI (LLM agents, MCP protocol) with practical fintech (algorithmic trading, backtesting). Its one-command setup, multi-broker support, and safety-first design (mandates, kill switches) lower the barrier for developers and traders to experiment with AI-driven trading, making it a compelling project for both communities.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Vibe-Trading demonstrates how to build a production-grade AI agent with real-world financial integrations. Developers can learn from its connector architecture, safety patterns (mandates, audit ledger), and MCP tool design. It's a reference for creating autonomous agents that interact with external APIs while maintaining user control.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward LLM-powered financial automation where safety and user consent are first-class concerns. The project's use of MCP (Model Context Protocol) for broker connectivity and its 'connector-first' design pattern could become a template for other agentic fintech tools. Developers should watch how the community handles risk and compliance in open-source trading agents.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Quantitative traders, algorithmic trading enthusiasts, AI developers interested in fintech, and researchers exploring LLM agents for financial decision-making. Also suitable for developers building multi-agent systems or integrating MCP with external services.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Trading involves financial risk. The project explicitly states that live trading is experimental and at user's own risk. Safety features (mandates, kill switches) are opt-in and may not prevent all losses. Users should thoroughly test with paper accounts before live deployment. The project is under active development, so APIs and features may change.

projects.name: MiroFish
projects.description: A Simple and Universal Swarm Intelligence Engine, Predicting Anything. 简洁通用的群体智能引擎，预测万物

llmSummary.oneLiner: MiroFish is a swarm intelligence engine that simulates multi-agent interactions in a digital twin world to predict real-world outcomes from news, policies, or financial signals.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Traditional prediction methods are static and lack the ability to model complex emergent behaviors from individual interactions. MiroFish provides a dynamic, multi-agent simulation sandbox where users can inject seed data and observe predicted futures through collective intelligence.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project has accumulated 64,467 stars and 10,065 forks, with a 24-hour star delta of 2,194 and a 7-day delta of 2,194, indicating sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), suggesting steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. The rule-based trend type is 'sustained_hot', and the project is categorized as an AI Agent Framework with a score of 66. This consistent momentum makes it a notable project in the multi-agent simulation space.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The last push was on 2026-05-24, but there is no recent release or commit within the last 7 days. The sustained star growth likely stems from ongoing community interest and word-of-mouth.
llmSummary.whyTrending: MiroFish combines swarm intelligence, multi-agent simulation, and LLMs to offer a practical tool for predicting anything from public opinion to financial trends. Its clear vision, live demo, and support from Shanda Group add credibility. The project's ability to handle both serious predictions (policy, finance) and creative simulations (novel endings) broadens its appeal.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: MiroFish demonstrates how to build a universal prediction engine using multi-agent systems and LLMs. Developers can learn from its architecture: seed extraction, GraphRAG, persona generation, dual-platform simulation, and report generation. The project is open-source and provides a quick start with both source code and Docker deployment.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, MiroFish signals a growing trend toward using multi-agent simulations for real-world prediction tasks. It shows that combining LLMs with agent-based modeling can produce actionable insights, and that open-source frameworks in this space are becoming more accessible and practical.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Decision-makers (policy, PR, finance), researchers in multi-agent systems, AI developers interested in swarm intelligence, and creative users who want to simulate 'what-if' scenarios.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 300 open issues, which may indicate active development but also potential instability. The README mentions high LLM API consumption, so running large simulations could be costly. The project relies on external services like Zep Cloud for memory, which may introduce dependencies. No recent commits or releases could mean development has slowed.

projects.description: Find vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, secrets, SBOM in containers, Kubernetes, code repositories, clouds and more

llmSummary.oneLiner: Trivy is a comprehensive, all-in-one security scanner for containers, Kubernetes, code, and cloud, detecting vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, secrets, and SBOMs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers and security teams need a single, fast, and reliable tool to scan multiple targets (containers, filesystems, Git repos, VMs, K8s) for various security issues (CVEs, IaC misconfigs, secrets, licenses) without juggling multiple scanners.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 35,760 stars, 368 stars in the last 24 hours, and a sustained_hot trend, Trivy is experiencing consistent, high-velocity growth. Its acceleration is low (1), indicating organic, steady adoption rather than a sudden spike. The project has a recent release and commits within 7 days, showing active maintenance. This is a mature, trusted tool that continues to gain momentum in the DevSecOps space.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits suggest ongoing improvements, but no external event (e.g., HN front page, major CVE) is evident from the data.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Trivy's all-in-one approach (vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, secrets, licenses) across multiple targets (containers, K8s, cloud, code) fills a critical need in DevSecOps. Its ease of use, broad coverage, and integration with CI/CD (GitHub Actions, VS Code, K8s operator) make it a go-to choice for security scanning.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Trivy is a must-have in any modern DevSecOps pipeline. It reduces toolchain complexity and provides a unified view of security posture. Developers should integrate it early in the development lifecycle to catch issues before deployment.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Trivy signals a shift toward consolidated security tooling. As AI-generated code and infrastructure become more common, automated scanning for vulnerabilities and misconfigurations will be essential. Trivy's support for IaC and secrets scanning positions it as a key component in AI-driven development workflows.
llmSummary.targetUsers: DevOps engineers, security engineers, platform engineers, developers working with containers, Kubernetes, cloud infrastructure, and CI/CD pipelines.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Trivy is a scanner, not a fixer — it reports issues but does not remediate them. Users must have processes to address findings. Also, as an open-source tool, support relies on community and commercial backing from Aqua Security.

projects.name: headroom
projects.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses prompts, tool outputs, logs, and RAG chunks by 60-95% before they reach an LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: LLM context windows fill up quickly with verbose tool outputs, logs, and RAG results, driving up costs and limiting agent effectiveness. Headroom solves this by compressing content locally before sending it to the LLM, using multiple algorithms (JSON, AST, ML) and a reversible compression scheme (CCR) so the LLM can retrieve originals on demand.
llmSummary.whyNow: Headroom is exploding in popularity with 12,941 stars and a sustained 3,316 stars/day over the last 3 days. The 24h delta of 3,299 stars and a 1.0 acceleration ratio indicate a steady, massive influx of attention rather than a sudden spike. This is a clear signal that the AI developer community is urgently seeking token optimization tools for agent workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and continuous commits suggest ongoing feature additions and polish, but the sustained high star velocity is likely driven by word-of-mouth and the growing need for cost-efficient AI agent deployments.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a critical pain point for anyone using AI coding agents: token costs and context limits. Its multi-mode support (library, proxy, MCP, agent wrap) makes it drop-in compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, and more. The promise of 60-95% token reduction with no accuracy loss is extremely compelling for both individual developers and enterprises.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding agents daily, Headroom can dramatically reduce your token spend and allow you to fit more context into each prompt. The reversible compression (CCR) ensures you never lose data. It's worth trying the 60-second install and running `headroom perf` to see savings on your own workloads.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local, agent-agnostic infrastructure layers that optimize LLM interactions. Headroom's cross-agent memory and shared context features point to a future where multiple agents collaborate efficiently without redundant token consumption. Developers should consider integrating such a compression layer early to future-proof their agent stacks.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers and teams using coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot CLI), RAG pipelines, or any LLM-based application where token costs and context limits are a concern. Also relevant for enterprise teams deploying multi-agent systems.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (12k stars but still early stage) with 173 open issues, indicating active development but potential instability. The compression algorithms may not work equally well for all content types; users should benchmark on their own data. The proxy mode introduces a local dependency that may not suit all environments.

It's a reference for creating autonomous agents that interact with external APIs while maintaining user control.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward LLM-powered financial automation where safety and user consent are first-class concerns. The project's use of MCP (Model Context Protocol) for broker connectivity and its 'connector-first' design pattern could become a template for other agentic fintech tools. Developers should watch how the community handles risk and compliance in open-source trading agents.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Quantitative traders, algorithmic trading enthusiasts, AI developers interested in fintech, and researchers exploring LLM agents for financial decision-making. Also suitable for developers building multi-agent systems or integrating MCP with external services.

watchlistMovements.name: headroom
watchlistMovements.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses prompts, tool outputs, logs, and RAG chunks by 60-95% before they reach an LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: LLM context windows fill up quickly with verbose tool outputs, logs, and RAG results, driving up costs and limiting agent effectiveness. Headroom solves this by compressing content locally before sending it to the LLM, using multiple algorithms (JSON, AST, ML) and a reversible compression scheme (CCR) so the LLM can retrieve originals on demand.
llmSummary.whyNow: Headroom is exploding in popularity with 12,941 stars and a sustained 3,316 stars/day over the last 3 days. The 24h delta of 3,299 stars and a 1.0 acceleration ratio indicate a steady, massive influx of attention rather than a sudden spike. This is a clear signal that the AI developer community is urgently seeking token optimization tools for agent workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and continuous commits suggest ongoing feature additions and polish, but the sustained high star velocity is likely driven by word-of-mouth and the growing need for cost-efficient AI agent deployments.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a critical pain point for anyone using AI coding agents: token costs and context limits. Its multi-mode support (library, proxy, MCP, agent wrap) makes it drop-in compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, and more. The promise of 60-95% token reduction with no accuracy loss is extremely compelling for both individual developers and enterprises.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding agents daily, Headroom can dramatically reduce your token spend and allow you to fit more context into each prompt. The reversible compression (CCR) ensures you never lose data. It's worth trying the 60-second install and running `headroom perf` to see savings on your own workloads.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local, agent-agnostic infrastructure layers that optimize LLM interactions. Headroom's cross-agent memory and shared context features point to a future where multiple agents collaborate efficiently without redundant token consumption. Developers should consider integrating such a compression layer early to future-proof their agent stacks.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers and teams using coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot CLI), RAG pipelines, or any LLM-based application where token costs and context limits are a concern. Also relevant for enterprise teams deploying multi-agent systems.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (12k stars but still early stage) with 173 open issues, indicating active development but potential instability. The compression algorithms may not work equally well for all content types; users should benchmark on their own data. The proxy mode introduces a local dependency that may not suit all environments.

items.title: NVIDIA CEO 黄仁勋访问首尔：与韩国共建 AI 未来
items.description: NVIDIA 创始人兼 CEO 黄仁勋本周到访首尔，会见韩国的 AI 建造者、主权基础设施合作伙伴及游戏社区。黄仁勋表示，为应对下半年繁忙的 AI 基础设施建设，需对齐 AI 供应链。他指出 Grace Blackwell 系统表现良好，Vera Rubin 已全面投产。他还强调机器人技术将成为韩国的下一个重要产业，并呼吁韩国投资 AI。

items.title: 腾讯高级执行副总裁汤道生：今年腾讯大部分代码都由 AI 生成
items.description: 在 6 月 5 日的腾讯云 AI 产业应用大会上，腾讯高级执行副总裁汤道生表示，今年腾讯大部分代码都由 AI 生成，工程师将更多时间用于架构设计，定期指导与修正 AI 输出。腾讯 2026 年 Q1 财报显示，重组后的 AI 研发团队重构了基础设施，并搭建了 Hy3 preview 模型。腾讯总裁刘炽平今年 3 月透露，去年腾讯在 AI 新产品上投入 180 亿元，今年投入至少翻倍。

items.title: NVIDIA CEO 黄仁勋访问首尔：与韩国共建 AI 未来
items.summary: NVIDIA 创始人兼 CEO 黄仁勋本周到访首尔，会见韩国的 AI 建造者、主权基础设施合作伙伴及游戏社区。黄仁勋表示，为应对下半年繁忙的 AI 基础设施建设，需对齐 AI 供应链。他指出 Grace Blackwell 系统表现良好，Vera Rubin 已全面投产。他还强调机器人技术将成为韩国的下一个重要产业，并呼吁韩国投资 AI。

normalizedKeys: title:腾讯高级执行副总裁汤道生 今年腾讯大部分代码都由 ai 生成

items.title: 腾讯高级执行副总裁汤道生：今年腾讯大部分代码都由 AI 生成
items.summary: 在 6 月 5 日的腾讯云 AI 产业应用大会上，腾讯高级执行副总裁汤道生表示，今年腾讯大部分代码都由 AI 生成，工程师将更多时间用于架构设计，定期指导与修正 AI 输出。腾讯 2026 年 Q1 财报显示，重组后的 AI 研发团队重构了基础设施，并搭建了 Hy3 preview 模型。腾讯总裁刘炽平今年 3 月透露，去年腾讯在 AI 新产品上投入 180 亿元，今年投入至少翻倍。

items.summary: Anthropic 发布报告称其最新 AI 模型已显现脱离人类控制迹象，呼吁全球暂缓前沿 AI 开发，以便社会制度建设和对齐研究跟上进展。该公司主张美国、中国等主要 AI 公司达成共识，发布可验证的规则，并类比"核武器不扩散条约"，但指出 AI 更难监管。该观点引发美国白宫部分官员不满，批评其夸大风险。Anthropic 计划未来数月召集各方探讨全球协调机制如何运作。

trendEntities.title: 马斯克谈SpaceX上市：正处大规模资本扩张期
normalizedKeys: title:马斯克谈spacex上市 正处大规模资本扩张期

items.title: 马斯克谈SpaceX上市：正处大规模资本扩张期
items.summary: 马斯克在JPMorgan活动上回应SpaceX上市问题：他已被建议上市近10年，自2014-2015年起SpaceX就已实现正现金流并自筹资金，之前的私募轮次实际是面向投资者和员工的流动性/回购轮次。当前不同之处在于SpaceX正进入显著资本增长阶段，计划发射约10万颗通信卫星（可能超10万颗），AI和机器人对带宽需求巨大，还将在太空中建设AI数据中心，马斯克认为这将成为AI扩张的主要手段。

trendEntities.title: Google Research 发布被动心率监测系统 PHRM
normalizedKeys: title:google research 发布被动心率监测系统 phrm

items.title: Google Research 发布被动心率监测系统 PHRM
items.summary: Google Research 开发了一种被动心率监测系统（PHRM），利用智能手机前置摄像头在日常使用中（人脸解锁后数秒内）捕捉面部视频，通过深度学习估算心率，平均绝对百分比误差（MAPE）低于10%（对比心电图金标准），满足各肤色人群的行业精度标准。系统将全天心率测量整合为每日静息心率（RHR），平均绝对误差（MAE）低于5 bpm（对比可穿戴设备）。研究同时发布了迄今最大规模的公开智能手机视频数据集及预训练模型PHRM-mini，合格研究人员可申请访问。

trendEntities.title: Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety：面向全球企业AI的可定制多模态安全
normalizedKeys: title:nemotron 3 5 content safety 面向全球企业ai的可定制多模态安全

items.title: Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety：面向全球企业AI的可定制多模态安全
items.summary: Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety基于Gemma 3 4B IT，提供128K上下文窗口，支持用户提示、可选图像与助手响应的统一多模态安全评估。新增自定义策略执行，允许企业用自然语言定义专属安全规则；THINK模式可输出可审计的逐步推理痕迹。显式训练覆盖12种语言，并借助基座模型零样本泛化至约140种语言。输出提供低延迟二分类、带分类标签、THINK推理痕迹三种模式。安全分类遵循Aegis 2.0框架（13核心类别+10细分类别）。同步发布多模态、多语言安全数据集，可在8GB+ VRAM GPU上实时部署。

trendEntities.title: Boson AI 与 LMSYS 发布基于 SGLang-Omni 的 Higgs Audio v3 TTS 端到端服务
normalizedKeys: title:boson ai 与 lmsys 发布基于 sglang omni 的 higgs audio v3 tts 端到端服务

items.title: Boson AI 与 LMSYS 发布基于 SGLang-Omni 的 Higgs Audio v3 TTS 端到端服务
items.summary: Boson AI 与 LMSYS 联合推出基于 SGLang-Omni 推理框架的 Higgs Audio v3 TTS 端到端服务。该模型约 4B 参数，基于 Qwen3-4B 骨干，支持 100 种语言（内部评测覆盖 111 种），在 Seed-TTS、CV3、MiniMax-Multilingual 及 Higgs-Multilingual 零样本语音克隆任务中达到单字级 WER/CER。开发者可通过文本内控制标签实时调整情感（20+种）、风格、韵律（语速/音高/停顿）及音效。模型支持流式合成，文本未完整时即可开始生成语音并保持一致性。SGLang-Omni 专为多阶段生成模型设计，统一调度 AR 解码与轻量计算，实现低延迟推理。

normalizedKeys: title:alex imas 和 phil trammell agi 后什么仍然稀缺

items.title: Alex Imas 和 Phil Trammell：AGI 后什么仍然稀缺？
items.summary: 经济学家 Alex Imas 和 Phil Trammell 指出，AGI 时代机器人数量可以快速复制增长，但人类独特技能（以芭蕾舞演员为例）的数量保持不变，揭示了即使技术大幅进步，某些稀缺资源仍不可替代。

normalizedKeys: title:nex n2 pro 发布 基于 qwen3 5 的 397b moe 推理模型 性能达 gpt 5 5 水平

items.title: Nex-N2-Pro 发布：基于 Qwen3.5 的 397B MoE 推理模型，性能达 GPT-5.5 水平
items.summary: neolab 推出 Nex-N2-Pro，基于 Qwen3.5-397B-A17B，总参数 397B 的 MoE 推理模型，支持 262K 上下文与多模态（VLM），性能达到 GPT-5.5 和 Claude Opus 4.7 级别。模型可自动调节推理深度，减少 30-50% 思考 token 且无性能折损，在 Terminal Bench 2.1、GDPVal、SWE-Verified 上取得 SOTA。擅长智能体编码、深度搜索和工具使用，兼容 Claude Code、Cursor 等工具。硅基流动已提供 T+0 支持，前两周免费使用。

items.title: 腾讯混元联合人大开源PlanningBench评估框架
items.summary: 腾讯混元（Tencent Hunyuan）与中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院合作，开源PlanningBench--一个可扩展、可验证的LLM规划能力评估与训练框架。该框架包含30+真实世界规划任务，支持自动验证和训练。PlanningBench旨在推动LLM从"说"到"做"的规划能力发展。资源已发布于arXiv、GitHub及HuggingFace。

items.title: Nex-N2-Pro 发布：基于 Qwen3.5 的 397B MoE 推理模型，性能达 GPT-5.5 水平
items.summary: neolab 推出 Nex-N2-Pro，基于 Qwen3.5-397B-A17B，总参数 397B 的 MoE 推理模型，支持 262K 上下文与多模态（VLM），性能达到 GPT-5.5 和 Claude Opus 4.7 级别。模型可自动调节推理深度，减少 30-50% 思考 token 且无性能折损，在 Terminal Bench 2.1、GDPVal、SWE-Verified 上取得 SOTA。擅长智能体编码、深度搜索和工具使用，兼容 Claude Code、Cursor 等工具。硅基流动已提供 T+0 支持，前两周免费使用。

items.title: Boson AI 与 LMSYS 发布基于 SGLang-Omni 的 Higgs Audio v3 TTS 端到端服务
items.summary: Boson AI 与 LMSYS 联合推出基于 SGLang-Omni 推理框架的 Higgs Audio v3 TTS 端到端服务。该模型约 4B 参数，基于 Qwen3-4B 骨干，支持 100 种语言（内部评测覆盖 111 种），在 Seed-TTS、CV3、MiniMax-Multilingual 及 Higgs-Multilingual 零样本语音克隆任务中达到单字级 WER/CER。开发者可通过文本内控制标签实时调整情感（20+种）、风格、韵律（语速/音高/停顿）及音效。模型支持流式合成，文本未完整时即可开始生成语音并保持一致性。SGLang-Omni 专为多阶段生成模型设计，统一调度 AR 解码与轻量计算，实现低延迟推理。
